role_separation_note: SEPARATE TUF role from the C18 release role AND the sigstore-trust-root role (OQ-2 family). This role's key signs the verifier-side revocation list (vkernel_revocations.json) consumed by audit_bundle/revocation.py via an INJECTED revocation_root_resolver — the revocation-root pubkey is never embedded in the list itself. Distinct rotation cadence: re-pinned on revocation-key rotation, not coupled to C18 release cuts. WS-3's is_revoked verify logic is independent of this artifact (it uses an injected resolver + in-test fixture root); this file is the PRODUCTION distribution of that root.
comment_bootstrap_disposition: v0.4 bootstrap: the keyids/pubkeys below are software-generated ed25519 placeholders and the signatures are EMPTY. The production v0.3.0-rc1 ceremony regenerates with hardware-rooted keys (YubiKey FIDO2 + cloud KMS + cold-storage) per audit_bundle/extensions/_tuf_root/README.md and fills real signatures + the pinned revocation-list-signer fingerprint. The synthetic-marker RELEASE gate (tests/test_synthetic_c18_marker_gate.py) BLOCKS any release while this file (or root.json / sigstore_trust_root.json) still carries empty sigs or unfilled ceremony placeholders.

signed.bootstrap_note: Bootstrap revocation_root.json — software-generated ed25519 keypairs, empty signatures. Production ceremony replaces with hardware-rooted keys per _tuf_root/README.md.

rotation_policy.comment: 2-of-3 distinct approvers per rotation ceremony; root metadata expiry MUST be <= 90 days. Mirrors the C18 release-root rotation discipline (Pass-1 tightening item 14 sub-bullet 7). Hardware-rooted multi-site token holdings satisfy 'distinct' structurally at v0.3; humans-only interpretation deferred to v1.5+ per OQ-7.

honest_hedge: v0.4 ships the revocation-root role STRUCTURE — separate role file, 2-of-3 threshold, <=90-day expiry policy, the role_name the loader will assert. The actual Ed25519 public-key bytes + signatures + pinned signer fingerprint are filled at the C18 key ceremony and embedded into the OCI image at release-tag cut. Until then the synthetic-marker RELEASE gate keeps this from being promoted.
